mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate supply-side risk aversion among equity investors, causing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week during periods of tight monetary policy.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative following INDPRO contractions when FEDFUNDS is above 3.0%, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate creates real yield compression and erodes purchasing power, forcing equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and flat FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and wage growth that support corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS levels show no persistent positive correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in real GDP growth below 1.0% annualized signals an imminent economic slowdown that triggers rapid portfolio de-risking, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where GDPC1 growth drops below the 1.0% threshold, invalidating the slowdown signal.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during periods of low unemployment indicates capacity utilization strain that boosts corporate pricing power and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day returns when UNRATE is below 4.5%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 45% in this specific regime.
